DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-29-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THANH TRUONG 'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THIS UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE STATUTORY PROVISIONS ON WHICH THE MOVING PARTY RELIES ONLY PERMIT SANCTIONS FOR UNSUCCESSFULLY MAKING OR OPPOSING A MOTION, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OPPOSED THIS MOTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TONY SCHOENBERG; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC.'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2023.030(D)(3). THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE IS A MOTION FOR PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO WITHDRAW THAT WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE SCHULMAN ON OCTOBER 19, 2020. IN THAT MOTION, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL STATES THAT DESPITE ITS BEST EFFORTS, THEY HAVE LOST CONTACT WITH PLAINTIFF WHO HAS NOT RESPONDED TO ANY COMMUNICATION SINCE DECEMBER 18, 2018. FROM ALL OF THE EVIDENCE, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ABANDONED HIS LAWSUIT. CONTINUED MOTION PRACTICE WOULD BE FUTILE AND ONLY POSTPONE THE INEVITABLE THAT THE CASE SHOULD BE DISMISSED. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-15-2021 Vacated

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD RE: PLAINTIFF JAMES SHALAR.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion granted. No opposition filed.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order on the mandatory Judicial Council form which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc. Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanction
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Michael Maloney for Defendant, Thanh Luong Truong.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC.'s Motion For Terminating Sanction is granted. Plaintiff's complaint is dismissed pursuant to CCP section 2023.030(d)(3). The Court notes that there is a motion for Plaintiff's counsel to withdraw that will be heard by Judge Schulman on October 19, 2020. In that motion, Plaintiff's counsel states that despite its best efforts, they have lost contact with Plaintiff who has not responded to any communication since December 18, 2018. From all of the evidence, it appears that Plaintiff has abandoned his lawsuit. Continued motion practice would be futile and only postpone the inevitable that the case should be dismissed. The request for monetary sanctions is denied.

Court to prepare order.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Oints And Authorities; Declaration Of Michael D. Maloney
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THANH TRUONG 'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. This unopposed motion is granted. The request for sanctions is denied. The statutory provisions on which the moving party relies only permit sanctions for unsuccessfully making or opposing a motion, and Plaintiff has not opposed this motion.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the judge pro tem.

Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenberg; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff James Shalars Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff James Shalars Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. This unopposed motion is granted. The request for sanctions is denied. The statutory provisions on which the moving party relies only permit sanctions for unsuccessfully making or opposing a motion, and Plaintiff has not opposed this motion.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the judge pro tem.

Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenberg; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
